skill.name: angular-state-management
skill.description: Master modern Angular state management with Signals, NgRx, and RxJS. Use when setting up global state, managing component stores, choosing between state solutions, or migrating from legacy patterns.
skill.summary: Master modern Angular state management with Signals, NgRx, and RxJS. Comprehensive guide for AI agents and LLMs.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 70 potential issues. Manual review confirms all findings are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are triggered by markdown code fences and TypeScript template literals. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' is triggered by words like 'sha' in 'shared' and 'source'. Network-related findings are legitimate documentation URLs and code examples. This is a genuine Angular state management documentation skill with no security concerns.

content.value_statement: Angular state management is complex with multiple solutions. This skill provides decision frameworks and implementation patterns for Signals, NgRx, Component Stores, and RxJS patterns to build maintainable applications.
actual_capabilities: Provide decision frameworks for choosing between Signals, NgRx, Component Stores based on app complexity
Generate Signal-based service patterns for local and shared state
Create NgRx Store setup with actions, reducers, selectors, and effects
Implement NgRx SignalStore with computed values and methods
Show migration patterns from BehaviorSubject to modern Signals
Demonstrate bridging techniques between Signals and RxJS Observables
limitations: Does not execute code or modify existing projects
Does not provide runtime debugging or Chrome DevTools integration
Does not generate complete project scaffolding or CLI commands
Does not cover backend state management or database patterns

use_cases.title: Choose the Right State Solution
use_cases.description: Get guidance on when to use Signals for simple state, Component Stores for feature-scoped state, or NgRx Store for enterprise applications with complex flows.
use_cases.target_user: Angular developers deciding on state architecture

use_cases.title: Implement Modern Signal Patterns
use_cases.description: Learn to create Signal-based services with computed values, toSignal conversion from Observables, and proper immutability patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Developers migrating from RxJS BehaviorSubject to Signals

use_cases.title: Set Up NgRx SignalStore
use_cases.description: Configure NgRx SignalStore with withState, withMethods, withComputed for type-safe feature state management without boilerplate.
use_cases.target_user: Enterprise Angular developers building scalable apps

prompt_templates.title: Basic State Solution Query
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an Angular app with [describe your use case]. Should I use Signals, Component Store, or NgRx? What factors should influence my decision?

prompt_templates.title: Signal Service Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Signal-based service for managing [describe state]. Include writable signals, computed values, and methods for updating state.

prompt_templates.title: NgRx Store Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up NgRx Store for [describe feature]. Include actions with createActionGroup, a reducer, selectors, and effects for async operations.

prompt_templates.title: Migration from BehaviorSubject
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this RxJS BehaviorSubject service to modern Angular Signals. Show the before and after patterns with proper reactivity.

output_examples.input: When should I use Signals vs NgRx Store?
output: Use Signals when: Simple local state, shared state between few components, computed derived values, lightweight solutions needed
Use NgRx Store when: Complex cross-feature dependencies, time-travel debugging needed, strict unidirectional data flow required, large team coordination

output_examples.input: Create a user state service with Signals
output: Service includes: private writable signals, readonly public signals using asReadonly(), computed values for derived state, methods for state updates

best_practices: Prefer Signals for new Angular applications - they are the recommended modern approach
Use computed() for all derived state to ensure automatic updates
Colocate state with the feature that uses it rather than global state
Use NgRx when you need time-travel debugging, strict patterns, or enterprise coordination
anti_patterns: Avoid mutating signals directly - always use set() or update() methods
Do not over-globalize state - keep local when possible to reduce complexity
Avoid mixing RxJS and Signals without clear bridging patterns
Do not subscribe in components for state - use signals in templates directly

faq.question: What is the simplest state management in Angular?
faq.answer: Signals are the simplest. Use signal() for writable state and computed() for derived values. No extra libraries needed.

faq.question: When should I use NgRx over Signals?
faq.answer: Use NgRx for large applications with complex state flows, when you need time-travel debugging, or when multiple teams work on the same codebase.

faq.question: Can I mix Signals with RxJS?
faq.answer: Yes. Use toSignal() to convert Observables to Signals, and toObservable() to convert Signals to Observables. Angular provides built-in support.

faq.question: What is NgRx SignalStore?
faq.answer: SignalStore is a new NgRx pattern that provides reactive state with computed values and methods. It is lighter than traditional NgRx Store.

faq.question: How do I migrate from BehaviorSubject to Signals?
faq.answer: Replace BehaviorSubject with signal(). Replace .next() with .set() or .update(). Replace .asObservable() with .asReadonly().

faq.question: Does this skill write code for me?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill generates code examples and patterns. You can copy them into your project. It does not modify files directly.
